texto: If a woman had a problem in the 1950's and 1960's, she knew that something must be wrong with her marriage, or with herself. Other women were satisfied with their lives, she thought. What kind of a woman was she if she did not feel this mysterious fulfillment waxing the kitchen floor? She was so ashamed to admit her dissatisfaction that she never know how many other women shared it.

texto: In almost every professional field, in business and in the arts and sciences, women are still treated as second-class citizens. A girl should not expect special privileges because of her sex, but neither should she "adjust" to prejudice and discrimination

texto: A woman today who has no goal, no purpose, no ambition patterning her days into the future, making her stretch and grow beyond that small score of years in which her body can fill its biological function, is committing a kind of suicide.

frase_original: A woman today who has no goal, no purpose, no ambition patterning her days into the future, making her stretch and grow beyond that small score of years in which her body can fill its biological function, is committing a kind of suicide.

texto: The man who is extremely and dangerously hungry has no other interest but food. Capacities not useful for the satisfying of hunger are pushed into the background. 'But what happens to man's desires when there is plenty of food and his belly in chronically filled? At once, other (and higher) needs emerge and these, rather than the psychological hungers, dominate the organism.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Betty Friedan que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “The man who is extremely and dangerously hungry has no other interest but food. Capacities not us…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, food, hunger, com foco em hunger e men.
